findings.title: TeamPCP Trojanizes LiteLLM on PyPI — Persistent .pth Backdoor in 36% of Cloud Envs
findings.affected_technology: LiteLLM Python library (PyPI) — present in 36% of cloud environments
findings.summary: TeamPCP poisoned LiteLLM 1.82.7/1.82.8 on PyPI (March 24) with a .pth-based backdoor that runs on every Python invocation to exfiltrate cloud creds, SSH keys, and K8s configs; LiteLLM present in 36% of cloud environments.

findings.title: PolyShell Magento Mass Exploitation — 56.7% of Vulnerable Stores Attacked, WebRTC Skimmer Variant
findings.summary: Active PolyShell exploitation now targets 56.7% of vulnerable Magento stores since March 19 with a newly discovered WebRTC payment skimmer variant enabling stealthy card data exfiltration via WebRTC data channels; no patch yet available for stable Magento releases.

findings.title: CVE-2026-33017 (Langflow RCE) Added to CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
findings.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-33017 (Langflow pre-auth RCE, CVSS 9.8) to the KEV catalog on March 25 with a mandatory federal remediation deadline, requiring all federal agencies and contractors with exposed Langflow instances to patch or disconnect immediately.

findings.title: TP-Link Archer NX Critical Auth Bypass — Unauthenticated Firmware Upload (CVE-2025-15517)
findings.summary: TP-Link disclosed critical auth bypass CVE-2025-15517 in Archer NX routers allowing unauthenticated firmware upload, bundled with hardcoded crypto key (CVE-2025-15605) and two command injection CVEs; mass-exploitation risk is high given TP-Link's ubiquitous SOHO footprint.

findings.title: BIND 9 Quad-CVE Patch Day — Remote DoS on DNS Resolvers via Malicious DNSSEC Zones
findings.summary: ISC disclosed four BIND 9 flaws on March 25 including CVE-2026-1519 (malicious DNSSEC zone causes excessive CPU) and CVE-2026-3104 (crafted domain causes unbounded memory leak/OOM crash), all remotely exploitable, no auth required on resolvers; patch to BIND 9.18.47, 9.20.21, or 9.21.20.

findings.title: Node.js High-Severity Security Releases — HTTP Server Crash via __proto__ Header, TLS SNI DoS
findings.summary: Node.js patched two High issues on March 24: CVE-2026-21710 (__proto__ header in HTTP requests crashes any Node.js HTTP server with uncatchable TypeError) and CVE-2026-21637 (TLS SNICallback exception crashes TLS servers), plus five Medium CVEs; all LTS lines (20.x–25.x) affected.

findings.title: Apple iOS/macOS 26.4 — 30+ CVEs Including WiFi MITM (KRACK Researcher) and WebKit UAF
findings.summary: Apple released iOS/macOS 26.4 on March 24 patching 30+ CVEs; standouts are CVE-2026-28865 (WiFi MITM by KRACK researcher Mathy Vanhoef) and CVE-2026-28879 (WebKit UAF); no in-the-wild exploitation yet but update all devices immediately.

noted.summary: Three Squid ICP request-handling DoS advisories (SQUID-2026:1/2/3) published March 25 — denial of service via malformed ICP packets requiring no authentication; patches released, no active exploitation reported.
Xen Security Advisory 482 (March 24) — Linux privcmd driver can circumvent kernel lockdown protections in Xen guest environments; patch available, relevant to Xen virtualization infrastructure.
Two memory-safety vulnerabilities in libfuse's io_uring interface disclosed March 25 — use-after-free and NULL dereference; affects Linux systems using FUSE filesystems via io_uring, patch available.
ISC disclosed CVE-2026-3608 in Kea DHCP server on March 25; technical details limited but patch available and proactive update recommended for organizations running Kea.
